name: Future Technology
description: A dark, high-contrast technology foresight system with electric-blue data accents, modular grids, and executive insight panels.
style_guide:
# Future Technology

## Visual concept

This is an English-language strategic foresight style for technology roadmaps, innovation portfolios, AI strategy, investment theses, and transformation programs. The visual language is dark, technical, and editorial without becoming a sci-fi poster: nearly black surfaces create focus, electric blue establishes the action layer, and cyan or violet are used sparingly to distinguish categories. Pages should feel like a decisive technology briefing with an explicit claim, measurable evidence, and a clear implication.

## Canvas, grid, and safe area

- Use a fixed `1280 × 720 px` canvas in a 16:9 ratio.
- Keep `56–72 px` safe margins. Decorative corner brackets may extend into the margin, but all text and data must remain inside it.
- Use a 12-column grid with `20–24 px` gutters. Use 6/6 splits for narrative-plus-analysis, 7/5 splits for chart-plus-insight, and 2×2 or 2×3 grids for trend cards.
- Maintain a consistent top metadata line: a small blue section label on the left and date, chapter, or report code on the right.
- Use a thin divider or grid boundary beneath the title area. The footer is a quiet gray source label on the left and a blue page number on the right.
- Background grids and geometric decoration must stay at low contrast. They should support alignment, never reduce text legibility.

## Color system

### Core colors

- Void black: `#0B0D0F`. Use as the primary slide background.
- Panel black: `#11151A`. Use for cards, chart wells, and secondary surfaces.
- Electric blue: `#1683FF`. Use for titles, active rails, progress bars, key numbers, section rules, and primary chart series.
- Signal cyan: `#17D3E3`. Use for a secondary metric or category when the data requires a clear second signal.
- Insight violet: `#5546FF`. Use sparingly for a third category, a strategic conclusion, or a distinct conceptual layer.

### Supporting colors

- Text white: `#F4F7FA`. Use for primary headlines and high-priority values.
- Text gray: `#8D969F`. Use for body copy, subtitles, labels, and metadata.
- Muted gray: `#4D555D`. Use for inactive rails, grid lines, and low-priority annotations.
- Blue wash: `#10223D`. Use behind selected KPI cards or insight blocks.
- Border blue: `#183B66`. Use for one-pixel borders and outlined controls.

### Usage rules

- Electric blue is the main interaction and emphasis color. It should be visually dominant without becoming a full-page fill.
- Keep most surfaces in Void black or Panel black. Do not use white cards or light backgrounds.
- Use cyan and violet only when they carry a data or structural meaning; do not use them as decoration.
- Avoid red, green, orange, and gold unless the content explicitly requires a status or risk semantic. If used, desaturate them and keep them subordinate to blue.
- Do not use gradients in text or data bars. A very subtle blue atmospheric glow may sit behind a hero object, but it must not reduce contrast.

## Typography

- Primary font: `Inter` or `Arial`; fallback: `Helvetica Neue, system-ui, sans-serif`.
- Use a heavy, condensed-feeling sans-serif for display text and a clean regular weight for explanations.
- Cover title: `58–84 px`, weight `800–900`, line-height `0.95–1.02`, uppercase is allowed for short titles.
- Page title: `40–54 px`, weight `800–900`, line-height `1.0–1.08`; emphasize one keyword in Electric blue when useful.
- Section label: `13–17 px`, weight `600–700`, uppercase with `2–4 px` tracking, Electric blue.
- Hero metric: `52–88 px`, weight `800–900`, Text white or Electric blue.
- Card title: `19–25 px`, weight `700–800`, Text white.
- Body copy: `16–19 px`, weight `400–500`, line-height `1.45–1.6`, Text gray.
- Chart labels and legends: `13–16 px`; sources and page metadata: `11–13 px`, Muted gray.
- Do not use long all-caps paragraphs. Reserve uppercase for labels, short headings, and compact data categories.

## Layout and component language

- Cards use Panel black backgrounds, `1 px` Border blue or Muted gray borders, square or `4–8 px` corners, and no heavy drop shadow.
- Use a `4–6 px` Electric blue rail on the left or top of an active insight card.
- Use thin outlined icon boxes, `48–64 px` square, with blue stroke icons and a faint Blue wash interior. Keep one icon family throughout the deck.
- KPI rows use a dark panel, a large value, a small uppercase label, and a short implication. Group related KPIs in a single bordered module.
- Trend cards may carry a faint two-digit index (`01`, `02`) in Muted gray at the top right. It must remain subordinate to the title.
- Use corner brackets, small nodes, and fine connector lines only as a restrained framing system. Never scatter them randomly.

## Charts and data

- Prefer horizontal progress bars, grouped bars, stacked bars, line charts, radar charts, and donut charts with direct labels.
- Electric blue is the primary series; Signal cyan is the secondary series; Insight violet is the tertiary series. Use muted gray for baselines and inactive portions.
- Progress bars use a dark inactive rail and a solid blue active fill with `8–12 px` height. Values sit at the far right in Electric blue or Text white.
- Chart grids use `#20262D` or Muted gray at low opacity. Keep axis labels gray and remove unnecessary visual noise.
- Donut and radar charts require a direct total, unit, or conclusion. Do not leave a chart as an unexplained visual.
- Every chart needs a period, unit, estimate marker when relevant, and source note. A data visualization should support one decision or conclusion per page.

## Page variations

- Cover: Void black background, centered or left-aligned white title, a blue report subtitle, subtle corner brackets, and one restrained “start” or thesis line.
- Core trends: a 2×2 grid of trend cards with blue icon boxes, short labels, one-line category tags, and two-sentence descriptions.
- Impact analysis: a narrative block on the left, a vertical list of quantified impact areas in the center, and one radar or comparison chart on the right.
- Human and AI: a split page with a large thesis statement on the left and a connected capability list on the right; use a faint oversized index number as a background anchor.
- Adoption metrics: a large list of horizontal progress bars on the left and a bordered Key Insight card with supporting KPIs on the right.
- Capital flow: a dominant donut chart on the left, an itemized allocation list on the right, and a compact market-trend conclusion below it.

## Imagery and icons

- Use diagrams, abstract technical systems, code or infrastructure motifs, and clearly relevant technology photography. Avoid generic neon stock imagery.
- Keep image treatment dark and controlled. Preserve inspectable details and do not add a heavy blur or opaque color wash.
- Icons should be simple line or solid symbols, consistently sized and rendered in Electric blue, Signal cyan, or Insight violet according to meaning.

## Avoid

- Do not use white backgrounds, pastel cards, glossy 3D effects, rainbow palettes, or decorative gradients.
- Do not turn every page into a dashboard. Narrative pages need a clear statement and enough breathing room.
- Do not make body copy too dim to read against Void black. Text gray must remain comfortably legible.
- Do not use more than three saturated data colors on one page.
- Do not add futuristic decoration that has no alignment, data, or semantic purpose.
